title: fix: bake Chromium into sandbox Dockerfile, remove runtime playwright install
description:
Onboard hangs forever on 'Installing Chromium via Playwright'. Root cause: runtime install runs inside sandbox whose deny-by-default policy blocks cdn.playwright.dev; runCapture has no timeout so execSync hangs; pipe through 'openshell sandbox connect' leaves zombie ssh-proxy children.

Fix:
1. cli/Dockerfile — install Playwright + Chromium during image build (network is available at build time, outside sandbox policy). Add after the npm install -g openclaw step, while still USER root:
   RUN npm install -g playwright@latest \
       && npx playwright install chromium \
       && npx playwright install-deps chromium \
       && CHROME=$(find /root/.cache/ms-playwright -name chrome-headless-shell -type f | head -1) \
       && CHROME_DIR=$(dirname $CHROME) \
       && cp -r $CHROME_DIR /opt/chromium-headless \
       && chmod -R 755 /opt/chromium-headless
   Use headless shell path /opt/chromium-headless/chrome-headless-shell.

2. cli/bin/lib/onboard.js — rewrite installBrowser() (lines 724-776) to verify-only:
   - Check /opt/chromium-headless/chrome-headless-shell exists via sandboxExec('test -x ...')
   - If present: call configureBrowser() with that path
   - If missing: print warning, do NOT attempt download, continue (non-fatal)
   - Remove all 'npx playwright install' calls and the /root/.cache find logic
   - Rename step label from 'Installing browser' to 'Configuring browser'

3. cli/bin/lib/runner.js — add timeout pass-through to runCapture (defense in depth):
   execSync(cmd, { ..., timeout: opts.timeout }). Let caller opt in.

Acceptance:
- diffract onboard completes past the browser step without hanging even if sandbox network fully blocked
- Chromium binary is usable from the sandbox user (chmod 755)
- openclaw.json browser config updated with correct executablePath
- Existing sandboxes without chromium baked in still proceed with a warning, not a hang
closeReason: Merged overstory/onboard-chromium-builder/Diffraction-1aa2

title: fix: sandbox delete UI shows stale state, no verification of actual removal
description:
ui/src/app/sandboxes/page.tsx:27-35 fires DELETE /api/sandboxes/:name, ignores the {deleted: boolean} response body, and immediately refetches. Actual k8s pod teardown is async (phase Deleting → gone takes 30s+), so the row stays visible until the 5s polling interval eventually catches up. If delete fails server-side the user sees no error.

Fix:
1. ui/src/lib/api.ts deleteSandbox already returns {deleted: boolean}. No change needed.
2. ui/src/app/sandboxes/page.tsx handleDelete:
   - Check the response: if !result.deleted, alert and return.
   - Mark the sandbox as pending-delete in local state (add a Set<string> 'deleting' to state) so the row shows a 'Deleting...' indicator and the delete button disabled.
   - Poll every 2s (up to ~60s) calling listSandboxes(); when the name disappears from the list, remove from the deleting set and refresh.
   - On timeout, alert 'Delete still pending — refresh to check'.
3. Render: rows whose name is in the deleting set get opacity-60 + spinner + 'Deleting' label where the age column normally sits; delete button disabled.

Acceptance:
- Delete button triggers immediate visual feedback (row dims, spinner)
- Row disappears only when sandbox actually gone from backend list
- Failed delete shows error
- 'cd ui && npm run build' clean
- 'npx tsc --noEmit -p ui/tsconfig.json' clean

File scope (narrow): ui/src/app/sandboxes/page.tsx only. Do not touch api.ts or other pages.
closeReason: Merged overstory/sandbox-delete-ui-builder-v2/Diffraction-e024 (bcb0408)

title: fix: POST /api/sandboxes broken — wrong repo path, invalid provider mapping, missing envs, no status feedback
description:
The UI 'Create Sandbox' button silently fails because api/routes/sandboxes.js:36-74 has multiple bugs. POST /api/sandboxes fires a detached onboard process, returns 202 immediately, and the onboard dies instantly with no feedback to the UI.

Bugs:
1. Wrong repo path (line 52): 'cd /root/diffract' — real path on VPS is /root/.diffract/repo. cd fails, subshell exits, spawn silently dies.
2. Invalid provider mapping (line 40): UI sends provider='nvidia'|'openai'|'anthropic'|'gemini'. cli/bin/lib/onboard.js:338 getNonInteractiveProvider() only accepts 'cloud'|'ollama'|'vllm'|'nim'. Any cloud provider name → process.exit(1).
3. Missing required env vars: NVIDIA_API_KEY (needed for cloud provider), DIFFRACT_DOMAIN (needed for Caddy allowedOrigins). These live in /root/.env on VPS.
4. Policy presets ignored: UI sends spec.policy_presets[], backend never reads it. onboard.js:1116 expects DIFFRACTION_POLICY_MODE=custom and DIFFRACTION_POLICY_PRESETS=comma,list.
5. Fire-and-forget: no way for UI to discover onboard failure. The spawn is detached with stdio ignore — if it dies, the sandbox detail page polls getSandbox forever and never learns.
6. No concurrency lock: two POSTs in quick succession race.

Fix plan (single file: api/routes/sandboxes.js):

A. POST / handler:
   - Correct path: cd /root/.diffract/repo (not /root/diffract)
   - Map UI provider → onboard provider: any of nvidia/openai/anthropic/gemini → 'cloud'; 'ollama' → 'ollama'; 'vllm' → 'vllm'; 'nim' → 'nim'. Keep the cloud-provider type as a separate spec field for later (stored in sandbox settings after onboard completes) if needed; for now just collapse to 'cloud'.
   - Inject env vars from process.env: NVIDIA_API_KEY, DIFFRACT_DOMAIN. Fall back to reading /root/.env if not in process env. Do NOT log the key values.
   - If spec.policy_presets is a non-empty array, add DIFFRACTION_POLICY_MODE=custom and DIFFRACTION_POLICY_PRESETS=<comma-join>. Otherwise let onboard default to 'suggested'.
   - Simple in-memory lock: const activeOnboards = new Map<name, {startedAt, logFile, pid}>. Reject 409 if name is already active.
   - Still fire detached (onboard takes 3-5 min), but record the child PID into the lock.

B. New GET /:name/onboard-status endpoint:
   - Reads the tail of /tmp/diffract-onboard-<name>.log (last 100 lines) via fs.readFile.
   - Returns { active: bool, exitCode: number|null, startedAt, elapsedMs, tail: string[] }.
   - If the lock has a pid, check if the process is alive via process.kill(pid, 0) (no-op signal). If alive → active:true. If not alive → check log tail for success markers ('Onboard complete' or 'provisioning failed') and report exitCode accordingly, then remove from lock.

C. UI side (ui/src/app/sandboxes/[name]/page.tsx — OUT OF SCOPE for this fix, note in report only): the detail page should poll the new onboard-status endpoint during the 'provisioning' phase to surface errors. File a follow-up issue for it.

Acceptance:
- POST /api/sandboxes with {name, spec:{provider:'nvidia', model:'nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b'}} on VPS successfully starts onboard and returns 202.
- The spawned child process actually stays alive past 2 seconds (tail the log file to verify).
- GET /api/sandboxes/<name>/onboard-status returns the tail and active state.
- Sending the same name twice while active returns 409.
- node -c api/routes/sandboxes.js clean.
- No regression to list/get/delete routes.

File scope (NARROW): api/routes/sandboxes.js only. Do NOT touch grpc-client, UI files, or cli/. Do NOT add dependencies.
closeReason: Merged POST /api/sandboxes fixes

title: install.sh: pin OPENSHELL_VERSION on correct side of pipe
description:
install.sh line 154 has a bug:

OPENSHELL_VERSION="0.0.21" curl -LsSf https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NVIDIA/OpenShell/main/install.sh | sh

The env var is set only for the curl process — bash does NOT propagate it to the piped 'sh'. Result: the OpenShell installer falls back to 'latest' (currently 0.0.22 which has port forward regression AND k3s gateway init failures).

Also: the OpenShell release tags use a 'v' prefix (v0.0.21), so the version string must be 'v0.0.21' not '0.0.21'.

Fix: move the env var to the sh side of the pipe, with the v prefix:

curl -LsSf https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NVIDIA/OpenShell/main/install.sh | OPENSHELL_VERSION=v0.0.21 sh

Files: install.sh (line ~154). Quality gates: bash -n install.sh.
closeReason: OPENSHELL_VERSION pinned correctly on sh side with v prefix

title: Replace /tmp log-file onboard job model with SQLite job table + SSE
description:
Current: api/routes/sandboxes.js spawns 'diffract onboard' as detached bash, tracks PID in in-memory activeOnboards Map, tails /tmp/diffract-onboard-<name>.log and infers status by grepping for 'complete'/'Error:'. State lost on API restart. Concurrent onboards collide.

Replace with: SQLite job table at ~/.diffract/jobs.db with columns (id, sandbox_name, status, started_at, finished_at, exit_code, log_path). Worker pool with concurrency=1 per host. cli/bin/lib/onboard.js emits structured step events ({step, status, ts}) written to job row. UI subscribes to /api/events SSE stream instead of polling /onboard-status every 3s.

Files: api/lib/jobs.js (new), api/routes/sandboxes.js, api/routes/events.js (new), cli/bin/lib/onboard.js, ui/src/app/sandboxes/[name]/page.tsx, ui/src/lib/api.ts.
closeReason: SQLite job table + SSE replaces in-memory map and log scraping

title: mTLS cert hot-reload in api/lib/grpc-client.js
description:
Current: gRPC client loads ca.crt/tls.crt/tls.key once at module load. When openshell gateway destroy+start regenerates certs (e.g. during onboard step 2), the API keeps using stale certs and every gRPC call fails with 'certificate signature failure'. Workaround today is manual API restart.

Fix: watch /root/.config/openshell/gateways/diffract/mtls/ with fs.watch (recursive); on change, rebuild grpc.credentials.createSsl() and swap the active client. Add a debounce (1s) to coalesce burst writes during cert rotation.

Files: api/lib/grpc-client.js. Quality gates: existing api tests pass; manual test: destroy gateway → start → confirm /api/sandboxes works without API restart.
closeReason: mTLS cert hot-reload via fs.watch + 1s debounce

title: Rewrite cli/bin/lib/onboard.js as explicit state machine
description:
Current: 7 imperative steps (preflight, gateway, sandbox, provider_selection, inference, openclaw, policies) with hand-rolled resumability via onboard-session.json. Bugs keep leaking: stale session blocks retry, browser step hangs, openshell sandbox connect PTY hang on step 7, NVIDIA_API_KEY check after spawning all the prep work. Resume logic is scattered.

Rewrite using XState (or a small custom FSM if XState is too heavy). Each state declares: entry action, exit action, timeout, onError transition, idempotency contract. Resume logic falls out of the machine — load persisted state, transition into the saved state. Each step gets a unit test covering happy path + timeout + error rollback.

Files: cli/bin/lib/onboard.js, cli/bin/lib/onboard-machine.js (new), cli/test/onboard-machine.test.js (new). Quality gates: all existing onboard tests pass; new state-machine tests cover each step's contracts.
closeReason: Onboard rewritten as FSM with structured event callback

title: Replace Express API bridge with Fastify + Zod schemas
description:
Current: api/ is hand-rolled Express, no schema validation, hardcoded shell-escaping in sandboxes.js, execSync everywhere, no shared types with UI. Routes silently accept any body. Shell-injection surface in restart-gateway endpoint (sandbox name interpolated into bash).

Rewrite with Fastify + Zod schemas at every route boundary. Define request/response types in api/schemas/ and re-export to ui/src/lib/api.ts so UI gets typed clients. Eliminate execSync; talk to gateway via existing grpc-client. For sandbox-internal commands, spawn with array args (no shell), never string interpolation.

Files: api/server.js, api/routes/*.js, api/schemas/*.ts (new), ui/src/lib/api.ts. Quality gates: api tests rewritten + pass; cd ui && npm run build.

title: Caddy admin API config patches instead of Caddyfile regeneration
description:
Current: api/routes/gateway-routes.js POST /sync rewrites the entire /etc/caddy/Caddyfile from a template every time a sandbox is created/deleted. Race-prone: two concurrent sandbox creates can stomp each other. Requires file write + systemctl reload.

Replace with Caddy's admin API at http://127.0.0.1:2019/config/. PATCH the routes array to add/remove individual sandbox routes. No file writes. No reload. Atomic.

Files: api/routes/gateway-routes.js, deploy/caddy/Caddyfile (enable admin endpoint, lock to localhost). Quality gates: api tests cover add+remove route; manual test on VPS confirms two concurrent creates both succeed.

title: Replace install.sh with Rust diffract-installer binary
description:
Current: install.sh is 8 imperative bash steps with no rollback, swallows errors with 2>/dev/null||true, has had multiple bugs (env var on wrong side of pipe, nvm PATH, cgroup config). Hard to test, hard to debug remotely. Assumes apt — won't work on RHEL/Alpine.

Replace with a static Rust binary diffract-installer (new crate or extension of crates/diffract-bootstrap) shipped via GitHub releases. Single curl → static binary → runs all install steps with proper error handling, dry-run mode, idempotent re-runs, structured logs, rollback on failure.

Files: crates/diffract-installer/ (new), install.sh (replace body with single curl + exec), .github/workflows/release-installer.yml (new). Quality gates: cargo test, cargo clippy, integration test on fresh Ubuntu Docker container.

title: OIDC SSO auth replacing bcrypt-in-JSON credentials
description:
Current: /root/.diffract/credentials.json holds bcrypt password hash. No SSO, no MFA, no per-user RBAC enforcement at gRPC layer (UI checks but the API can be hit directly). Single admin user.

Add OIDC support: tenants configure their IdP (Keycloak/Auth0/Okta/Google Workspace). UI redirects to IdP for login, receives signed token, presents to API on every request. API verifies token signature + claims. Per-sandbox RBAC enforced at gRPC interceptor, not just UI guards. Keep bcrypt as fallback for first-run/no-IdP installs.

Files: api/lib/auth.js, api/middleware/oidc.js (new), ui/src/app/login/page.tsx, ui/src/lib/auth.ts, diffract.yaml.example (oidc section). Quality gates: api tests cover token verify happy path + expired + bad signature; manual test against a Keycloak Docker container.

description:
Current: cli/test/ has unit tests, but the full flow (UI form → POST /api/sandboxes → onboard.js → openshell → sandbox up → chat iframe loads) has zero coverage. Every bug we hit this week (cert reload, NVIDIA key missing, browser hang, gateway PTY hang, gRPC SERVING vs SERVICE_STATUS_HEALTHY, Caddy missing route) would have been caught by one e2e test.

Add Playwright suite that:
1. Spins up a fresh Diffract install in a Docker container
2. Logs in via UI
3. Clicks Create Sandbox, fills form with NVIDIA key, submits
4. Waits for detail page to show 'ready' (not provisioning)
5. Verifies chat iframe loads /sandboxes/<name>/diffract_chat
6. Tears down

title: Federation: central control plane → N remote OpenShell nodes
description:
Current: one Diffract install = one host. Enterprises with multiple teams either run multiple installs (no central view) or cram everything on one VM (no isolation). Today's gRPC client is hardcoded to localhost:8080.

Architecture: split control plane (UI + API + auth + audit aggregator) from data plane (OpenShell + sandboxes). Each remote node registers with the control plane via signed JWT + mTLS. Sandbox scheduler picks node by load and policy constraints. Audit events stream from each node to control plane and merge into a single OCSF stream.

This is multi-week work. Pre-requisites: issues for OIDC (#7), Fastify rewrite (#4), and SQLite job table (#1) should land first because they're load-bearing.

Files: crates/diffract-controlplane/ (new), crates/diffract-node-agent/ (new), api/, proto/diffract.proto (federation messages), ui/src/app/nodes/ (new). Quality gates: cargo test, e2e against 2-node cluster.

title: onboard success must auto-sync caddy gateway routes
description: POST /api/sandboxes runs diffract onboard but never calls POST /api/gateway-routes/sync after success. Result: Caddy has no route for the new sandbox, iframe at /sandboxes/<name>/diffract_chat/ falls through to Next.js catch-all and loads the dashboard recursively. title: Restart Gateway button must also restart host port-forward
description: POST /api/sandboxes/:name/restart-gateway in api/routes/sandboxes.js:225 only restarts the OpenClaw process inside the sandbox. It does not check or restart the host-side openshell port-forward (127.0.0.1:18789 → sandbox). When the forward dies (observed: 'openshell forward list' shows 'dead'), curl http://127.0.0.1:18789/health returns 000 and the chat iframe is blank, but the in-sandbox gateway is fine. Restart Gateway button reports healthy:false in this state without surfacing the real cause. Fix: after killing/restarting the in-sandbox process, run 'openshell forward stop 18789 <name>' then 'openshell forward start 18789 <name> --background' before health-polling. Verified on VPS: manual forward restart fixed the blank iframe.

title: Watchdog: auto-restart dead openshell port-forward
description: Observed on VPS: smoke1 sandbox port-forward died silently (status='dead' in 'openshell forward list'). Direct curl 127.0.0.1:18789 returns connection refused. The Telegram bridge still works because it talks to the gateway via cluster-internal path, masking the failure. The host-side chat iframe goes blank with no user-facing error. Fix: extend scripts/gateway-watchdog.sh (or add a sibling forward-watchdog.sh) that polls 'openshell forward list' every 30s, detects 'dead' status, and runs 'openshell forward stop' + 'openshell forward start --background' to revive. Reference/guides/nemoclaw-setup-guide.md confirms this is a known fragility — guide explicitly tells users to manually re-run 'connect' when forward dies.

title: Caddy bare path returns 200 empty body for /sandboxes/<name>/diffract_chat
description: In api/routes/gateway-routes.js generateCaddyfile, the 'handle /sandboxes/<name>/diffract_chat/*' wildcard glob unexpectedly matches the bare parent path '/sandboxes/<name>/diffract_chat' (no trailing slash). The strip_prefix then leaves an empty path, which the gateway answers with 200 + content-length 0. The 'handle /sandboxes/<name>/diffract_chat { redir /sandboxes/<name>/diffract_chat/ permanent }' redirect handler that should fire instead is shadowed and never runs. Verified on VPS: bare URL returns 200/0 bytes, slash URL returns 2814 bytes of OpenClaw HTML. Fix: replace the two handles with a 'route' block that explicitly rewrites bare path to slash before strip_prefix, OR use a path matcher with not-equal exclusion. Reference: https://caddyserver.com/docs/caddyfile/matchers#path

title: Sandbox detail page must surface forward-dead state distinctly from gateway-dead
description: ui/src/app/sandboxes/[name]/page.tsx and chat-panel.tsx treat any non-200 from /sandboxes/<name>/diffract_chat the same. The user sees 'blank iframe' with no signal whether the in-sandbox gateway died or the host port-forward died. These need different remediations (Restart Gateway vs forward restart). Fix: API should expose a /api/sandboxes/:name/connectivity endpoint that returns {forward: 'running'|'dead', gateway: 'healthy'|'down'} and the page should render an actionable banner with the right button (Restart Forward / Restart Gateway / both).

title: gateway-token route must use openshell sandbox download, not connect+pipe
description:
api/routes/token.js:18 fetchTokenFromSandbox() pipes a python3 -c command into 'openshell sandbox connect <name>' and reads stdout. This silently fails because connect uses 'ssh -tt' which requires a PTY — piped stdin is consumed but the inner command never executes, returning empty output. Result: /api/gateway-token returns 404 'Token not found' even when the sandbox has a valid token. The chat panel's Token chip stays blank.

Fix: replace the connect+pipe approach with 'openshell sandbox download <name> /sandbox/.openclaw/openclaw.json <tmp>' which works without a PTY. Verified live on VPS — download succeeds, JSON parses to gateway.auth.token = f70ed.... The TOKEN_FILE cache then works.

Also: hardcode of 'python3 -c' relies on python3 being installed in the sandbox image — not guaranteed. Download approach removes that dependency entirely.

Also: function picks the FIRST sandbox via 'openshell sandbox list | head -1' even if request was for a specific name. The cache file is global, not per-sandbox. Multi-sandbox support needs per-name caching.

title: Caddyfile race: gateway-routes/sync overwrites diffraction.in marketing block
description:
Two systems write to /etc/caddy/Caddyfile and clobber each other:

1. POST /api/gateway-routes/sync (api/routes/gateway-routes.js generateCaddyfile) writes the full file with dynamic sandbox routes + srv1534809.hstgr.cloud block
2. website/deploy.sh appends a 'diffraction.in, www.diffraction.in' marketing block via 'echo $BLOCK >> /etc/caddy/Caddyfile' if not already present

Observed on VPS today: after a service restart something rewrote the Caddyfile with the marketing block at top BUT dropped all dynamic /sandboxes/<name>/diffract_chat routes. Result: chat URL fell through to Next.js catch-all and rendered the dashboard recursively. Manual POST /api/gateway-routes/sync restored it but also the next deploy.sh run will lose the marketing block again.

Fix: split responsibilities. generateCaddyfile() should write to /etc/caddy/conf.d/sandboxes.conf and only manage that file. The main /etc/caddy/Caddyfile should have a static 'import /etc/caddy/conf.d/*.conf' and the diffraction.in + srv1534809 srv blocks. Neither side overwrites the other. systemctl reload caddy still picks up both via the import directive (which is already present in current Caddyfile per today's inspection).

Verified live: today's regenerated Caddyfile already has 'import /etc/caddy/conf.d/*.conf' at line 13 — the import infra exists, generateCaddyfile just doesn't use it.

title: restart-gateway execSync must set PATH for openshell binary
description: api/routes/sandboxes.js:269-275 calls execSync('openshell forward stop/start ...') with no env override. title: openshell forward: add auto-reconnect on stream drop

title: Permanent fix: openclaw.json integrity hash stays in sync with legitimate config edits

title: Permanent gateway bridge: replace openshell forward with supervised kpf+socat + systemd persistence
description:
RECURRING PRODUCTION BUG. Chat iframe goes 502 every few hours because openshell forward ssh -f tunnel silently drops (TCP still accepts, HTTP empty reply). Forward-watchdog only does PID check so never catches it. Restart Gateway button also calls openshell forward start which is unreliable. This must be fixed permanently — no more manual intervention.

PROVEN WORKING BYPASS (tested live on smoke1 VPS, currently running):
1. docker exec openshell-cluster-diffract kubectl port-forward -n openshell <sandbox> 18789:18789 --address 0.0.0.0  (via nohup+disown on host)
2. socat TCP-LISTEN:18789,bind=127.0.0.1,fork,reuseaddr TCP:172.18.0.2:18789  (172.18.0.2 = cluster container bridge IP)
3. Caddy → 127.0.0.1:18789 (socat) → cluster bridge → kpf → gateway. Chat works 200 end-to-end.

DELIVERABLES:

A. scripts/diffract-gateway-bridge.sh (NEW)
   - Template script takes SANDBOX arg. Supervises both kpf + socat.
   - Discovers cluster container name (currently openshell-cluster-diffract) and bridge IP dynamically via docker inspect.
   - Runs HTTP probe loop (curl -m 3 http://127.0.0.1:18789/ every 10s). On failure: kill both processes, restart both.
   - Logs to /var/log/diffract-gateway-bridge-<sandbox>.log

B. deploy/systemd/diffract-gateway-bridge@.service (NEW)
   - Template unit: diffract-gateway-bridge@smoke1.service
   - ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/diffract-gateway-bridge %i
   - Restart=always, RestartSec=5
   - Enable on install so it survives reboot.

C. scripts/forward-watchdog.sh (PATCH)
   - Add HTTP probe layer. For each forward, try curl -m 3 http://127.0.0.1:$port/. If empty reply or timeout → log + restart the bridge systemd unit (not openshell forward).
   - Keep existing PID check as fallback.

D. api/routes/sandboxes.js restart-gateway (PATCH)
   - REMOVE 'openshell forward stop/start' calls (lines ~260-290, added in Diffraction-1dbf).
   - Instead, after in-sandbox gateway restart, do 'systemctl restart diffract-gateway-bridge@<sandbox>'.
   - Health poll on http://127.0.0.1:18789/ stays the same.

E. scripts/diffract-sandbox-entry.sh (PATCH — if needed)
   - Add /opt/chromium-headless to landlock exec allowlist so browser can spawn without EACCES. Currently browser is disabled on smoke1 as workaround.
   - Verify sandbox entry script mounts/allowlists /opt/chromium-headless for exec.

F. VPS DEPLOY
   - Install scripts/diffract-gateway-bridge.sh to /usr/local/bin/ on VPS
   - Install systemd unit, enable + start diffract-gateway-bridge@smoke1
   - Kill existing manual kpf + socat processes we started (docker exec kubectl port-forward... and socat on 18789)
   - Restart forward-watchdog with patched version
   - Deploy api/routes/sandboxes.js change + restart API
   - Test: click Restart Gateway in dashboard, verify chat comes back without manual intervention
   - Test: curl empty reply simulation — stop bridge, confirm watchdog revives within 30s

ACCEPTANCE:
- Clicking Restart Gateway in dashboard → chat iframe works within 15s (no manual steps)
- Killing the gateway process inside sandbox → watchdog revives in ≤30s
- VPS reboot → systemd starts bridge automatically → chat works after boot
- No more 502 Bad Gateway from openshell forward ssh -f deaths
- Mulch records under deployment domain for the pattern

CONTEXT:
- Current broken path: api/routes/sandboxes.js:260-290 runs openshell forward. Runtime patched to work via manual socat+kpf — this is the reference architecture for the permanent fix.
- VPS: 187.127.137.249, root / 24Hrushi@02hp, PATH=/root/.local/bin:$PATH
- Cluster container: openshell-cluster-diffract (bridge network openshell-cluster-diffract, gateway 172.18.0.1, container IP 172.18.0.2)
- Sandbox name on VPS: smoke1 (namespace openshell)
- Mulch: mx-6acba1, mx-74b5e3, mx-367908, mx-9ae549 all reference this recurring issue.

SCOPE:
- scripts/, deploy/systemd/, api/routes/
- Do NOT touch Reference/ (upstream)
- Separate from Diffraction-79dc which was trying to fix the upstream ssh supervisor — kpf bypass is preferred because it's enterprise-standard kubectl and doesn't depend on openshell internals.

A prior non-interactive onboard on VPS created smoke1 without inference credentials, leading to 'LLM request failed: network connection error' today because openclaw.json was left with placeholder inference.local baseUrl and no L7 proxy route. - Backend plumbing already exists: api/lib/jobs.js (SQLite job table, pattern mx-5a1934), api/routes/events.js (SSE stream, pattern mx-b3762f), cli/bin/lib/onboard.js (7-step FSM with resumable mode: non-interactive support). UI already has Next.js 16 / React 19 / shadcn. - Key architectural rule: API keys never enter the sandbox (convention mx-12279c, failure mx-92e962). Keys stored on host at ~/.diffract/credentials.json; L7 proxy injects at network layer via inference.local → real provider. GOAL: User clicks a 'Start Diffract Onboarding' button on /onboard route. --model --name --policies . Pipe stdout+stderr to job_events table. 5. — reads cli/models.json filtered by provider. Reuse model-registry.js if accessible. title: Fix destroy flow leaves stale Caddy routes + watchdog unit
description:
When a user destroys a sandbox via DELETE /api/deploy, the handler runs `diffract <sandbox> destroy --yes` but never cleans up downstream state. Two bugs interact:

**Bug 1** — `From Scrtch/diffract/src/app/api/deploy/route.ts`, `DELETE` handler (lines 207–228): no call to `openshell forward stop` and no call to `scripts/sync-sandbox-routes.sh`, so `/etc/caddy/conf.d/sandboxes.conf` keeps dead route blocks and `diffract-forward-watchdog@<sandbox>.service` stays running.

**Bug 2** — `From Scrtch/diffract/scripts/sync-sandbox-routes.sh`, lines ~28–34: when the sandbox list is empty, the script writes an empty-state conf and then `exit 0`, which skips the orphan-watchdog disable loop at the bottom. 4. Grep verification on the deployed VPS files confirms both fixes landed; dashboard `/api/status` still returns 200.

**Constraints**: do NOT run `diffract onboard` during verification (10–15 min). No git commits. No changes outside the two named files.

title: Landlock exec allowlist for /opt/chromium-headless in sandbox policy
description:
Per mulch mx-588772: enabling browser in openclaw.json with executablePath=/opt/chromium-headless/chrome-headless-shell crashes the gateway with 'Error: spawn /opt/chromium-headless/chrome-headless-shell EACCES'. File is chmod 755 root:root, no noexec mount — Landlock LSM blocks exec outside its allowlist.

policies/base.yaml line 30 already lists /opt/chromium-headless under filesystem_policy.read_only. Comment claims 'needs read+exec so browser can spawn' but the compiled Landlock ruleset almost certainly only grants READ_FILE/READ_DIR for a read_only entry, not EXECUTE. Hence EACCES at spawn.

Acceptance:
1. Inside a fresh sandbox with browser:enabled=true, /opt/chromium-headless/chrome-headless-shell spawns successfully (no EACCES, gateway does not crash).
2. Gateway exposes browser control on port 18791 and an agent browser_use tool call reaches the URL.
3. Other Landlock-restricted paths still deny exec (regression guard — don't grant exec globally on read_only).

Investigation pointers:
- Scout crates/diffract-policy and Reference/OpenShell policy-to-Landlock compiler for how filesystem_policy.read_only maps to Landlock access rights.
- Options: (a) add a new YAML field like filesystem_policy.execute (cleanest), (b) change read_only to grant READ+EXECUTE when path is a directory of binaries (risky, widens blast radius), (c) special-case /opt/chromium-headless (hacky).
- (a) is preferred — add a sibling 'execute' list to filesystem_policy and put /opt/chromium-headless there.
- Remove the now-misleading comment at policies/base.yaml:28-29.

Constraints:
- Must not weaken Landlock elsewhere.
- Rust edition 2024, MSRV 1.88. cargo clippy with pedantic + nursery is enabled.
- Run existing policy tests in crates/diffract-policy; add a new test covering exec permission compilation.
- Also check cli/ integration — no schema validator updates breaking onboard.
- Smoke test on VPS by rebuilding sandbox image and launching a new sandbox with browser enabled; 'diffract <name> connect' + test playwright spawn.

Out of scope:
- Any openclaw.json host-side write flow changes (mx-5db827 VFS cache) — track separately.
- Upstream OpenShell schema changes if this requires only diffract-layer overrides.
